title: AUTONOMOUS AEROBOT THERMAL CONTROL SYSTEM
what: Thermal analysis for an autonomous planetary aerobot
Passive thermal control system design following the NASA mission lifecycle
how: Churchill-Bernstein convection models and heat flow mapping
Variable geometry radiator with low emissivity coating (ε = 0.008)
Multi-objective optimization across mass, power, and cost constraints
results: 30 kW cooling and 11 kW heating requirements quantified across 350–400 K atmospheric profile
4 kg passive thermal system meeting $6.3M cost constraint
Design validated against full NASA mission lifecycle requirements
